title: Chen-Chvátal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: Every metric space on $n$ points, where $n \geq 2$, either has at least $n$ distinct lines or has a universal line.
context_text: Chen and Chvátal proposed this conjecture as a generalisation of the classical result that $n$ non-collinear points in the Euclidean plane determine at least $n$ distinct lines, using betweenness-based lines in arbitrary metric spaces. The paper establishes a slightly stronger version — $\ell(G) + \mathrm{br}(G) \geq |G|$ — for the hereditary graph class $\mathcal{C}$, which contains all chordal graphs and all distance-hereditary graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves a strengthened form of the conjecture ($\ell(G)+\mathrm{br}(G)\geq|G|$) for all connected graphs $G\in\mathcal{C}\setminus\mathcal{F}$, where $\mathcal{C}$ is a hereditary class strictly containing chordal and distance-hereditary graphs.
notes: Source is PDF; mathematical notation is simple and reads cleanly.
paper_title: A new class of graphs that satisfies the Chen-Chvátal Conjecture

title: Conjecture 2.2
statement_text: There is a finite set of graphs $F_0$ such that every connected graph $G \notin F_0$ either has a pendant edge or satisfies $\ell(G) + \mathrm{br}(G) \geq |G|$.
context_text: The authors observe that replacing a bridge by a path of arbitrary length in a counter-example to $\ell(G)+\mathrm{br}(G)\geq|G|$ yields infinitely many counter-examples, so finitely many minimal ones cannot account for all exceptions; they propose that graphs with a pendant edge should be excluded and that all remaining counter-examples come from a finite family. The three known minimal counter-examples with a bridge are depicted in Figure 2.
notes: Source is PDF; statement is cleanly readable.
paper_title: A new class of graphs that satisfies the Chen-Chvátal Conjecture

title: Conjecture 2.3
context_text: Here $\mathrm{ul}(G)$ denotes the number of pairs of vertices in $G$ that induce a universal line. This variant replaces the bridge count by the universal-line-pair count; it is stronger than the original Chen-Chvátal conjecture in that it does not dismiss graphs with universal lines as trivial, and no counter-example is known to this conjecture.
notes: Source is PDF; statement is cleanly readable.
paper_title: A new class of graphs that satisfies the Chen-Chvátal Conjecture

title: Open question on counter-examples to ℓ(G)+br(G)≥|G|
statement_text: It remains unknown whether all counter-examples to $\ell(G) + \mathrm{br}(G) \geq |G|$ can be obtained from a finite set of graphs by replacing a bridge by a path of arbitrary length.
context_text: The authors note (on the basis of an observation by Yori Zwols) that any counter-example containing a bridge generates infinitely many counter-examples by path-elongation. They ask whether this operation accounts for all counter-examples.
notes: Stated as 'it remains unknown' in running prose without a labelled environment.
paper_title: A new class of graphs that satisfies the Chen-Chvátal Conjecture
